item.title: 11:49 am, February 28, 2026
item.description: A little bit more axe wrangling, trying to get the big blocks in place on the upper and lower blades. Definitely needs a good old scuffing and weathering pass.

media:content.media:description: The background has been cropped out of this frontal shot of the mini so that I can see the values a bit more clearly (without the insane mess of wet palette and discarded paints which normally add a good degree of visual noise). A lower set of highlights have been added to the axe blades, green used to imply the sky/ground reflected light distinction.

item.title: 6:45 pm, February 27, 2026
item.description: Immediately sidetracked by non-metallic metal. The eventual headstone of every mini-painter.

media:content.media:description: Look. We can all agree that the tattoo is a huge and life-altering mistake that I’ll be paying for forever. So why not compound it with a massive ‘steel’ axe that will take just as long? In this shot the upper blade has been sketched in, it’s a bit too blue and not quite bright enough at the brightest point. It also definitely reads a bit ‘fantasy comic illustration’ rather than ‘real’. Which isn’t bad necessarily, but doesn’t quite fit the rest of the mini.

item.title: 5:53 pm, February 26, 2026
item.description: Finally starting into a slow expansion of the tattoo. This is going to take a billion years.

media:content.media:description: Tiny curly bits are being drawn around the central tattoo circle on the biggest left shoulder bump, white paint on dark blue. Approximately one one tenth of one percent compete. You can hear the tears falling gently, splish slash, just offscreen.

item.title: 8:28 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description: Starting to sketch out what a full-sleeve tattoo might look like for a fantasy dorf. Ever get the feeling you’ve bitten off absurdly more than you can chew?

media:content.media:description: Well. This is certainly going to be something. Probably not a success, as such. But _what_ a failure. Just _extravagant_ potential failure. A dark-blue and purple sleeve has been basecoated (if you’ll pardon the noun-verbing) on the left arm and verrry tentative little curve sketches have been started towards a triquetra on the deltoid.

item.title: 6:23 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description:
Why yes, I did just add a massively passive-agressive self-shaming /now page to my blog, thank you for asking.

https://fernseed.org/now/

media:content.media:description: A screenshot of a website ‘Right Now’ section showing ‘completed projects: 1 since April 29, 2012 (about one every 5,047 days)’. Genuinely expected a NaN result, so this is totally fine.

item.title: 4:46 pm, February 21, 2026
item.description: Beefy boi.

media:content.media:description: One (absurdly muscular) arm of the mini now has a range of tones from purple in deepest shadow through reddish browns to a small specular highlight of pure ivory on the side delt. Veins, as they say, are poppin’.

item.title: 1:19 pm, February 21, 2026
item.description:
Starting on the shadow-side arm fleshtones.

Aside: I will always attempt skin ‘from black’ in the future. If you build up in thin layers you get such a developing sense of translucency for free.

media:content.media:description: Angry-Face’s beefy right arm now has some paint on it. Thin layers of dark red and progressively lighter brown are being applied. The black primer still bleeds through the layers giving (hopefully? I think?) a sense of translucent skin-over-meat.

item.title: 10:39 am, February 21, 2026
item.description: A further ‘drama’ pass on the hair.

media:content.media:description: Trying to add contrast and colour variation with some ivory now added in the brightest highlight and purple in the shadows/bounce-light reflections.

item.title: 7:01 pm, February 20, 2026
item.description: Hairdressing intensifies

media:content.media:description: A topknot has been attached to the head of the mini, the same red-to-orange highlight pass has been applied.

item.title: 5:23 pm, February 20, 2026
item.description: Look at this absolute shambles of a workspace. Very much reflecting the mind behind it.

media:content.media:description: Acrylic paint pots of different sizes, oil paint tubes, paintbrushes, an airbrush and holder, putty modelling tools, bits of sprue, a covered wet palette, bits of paint-covered tissue, mugs of dirty paint water, all jumbled and crammed together as though by some passing neighbourhood madman. A mini stands in the midst, framed by a painting light and magnifying lenses at the top and a very worn faux-leather desk chair at the bottom.

item.title: 8:45 am, February 20, 2026
item.description: The thing currently being sold as artificial intelligence reads a lot like the Chinese Room problem, if written by Borges.

item.title: 7:12 pm, February 19, 2026
item.description: First beard(s) highlight pass sketched in.

media:content.media:description: A little red-to-orange pass across the main beard strands, most orange on the upwards-facing portions. The whole beard will probably need to get a lot brighter before we’re done.

item.title: 6:00 pm, February 19, 2026
item.description: Right. Let’s get started on that hair then. All of which is entirely composed of additional beards.

media:content.media:description: A dark red base coat has been applied to a beard larger than the average human torso. A start has been made in layering up towards orange on the many, overlapping moustache mini-beards.

item.title: 7:50 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: In contrast, the first ‘real’ (fake) highlight placement.

media:content.media:description: Ivory paint is added to the brown in stages to distract the eye from ‘real’ (pfft) light and imply a starker contrast. Too stark? Yes. Will it get starker? It will. Will that make it better in some way? Almost certainly not, no.

item.title: 6:32 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: Interestingly, at this kind of size, at this distance from my painting lamp, the real light reflection reads much more scale-appropriate, so it looks like I’ve been highlighting here when I really haven’t.

item.title: 6:18 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: Tiny eye dots, tiny teef dots, and a patchy first couple of base layers for the skin, over black. The fool’s not even using a zenithal.

media:content.media:description: A mostly black-primed chonky dorf. If you squint, you can see his squint. The head, lack of neck, and trapezius have an initial brown basecoat applied. This could take a while. And that’s coming from _this_ guy.

item.title: 6:36 pm, February 17, 2026
item.description:
Okay. Now hear me out. Have I abandoned yet another 80%* complete mini because every part of my flesh and skin and soul rebels at the thought of ever even looking at it again? Yes. Technically, yes.

But! I’ve now picked up a vastly larger, more fine-detailed display figure that will need exponentially more patience and skill and time to complete. I see this as a clear win.

*~40-80%

media:content.media:description: A whole new child appears! Bearded and girthy! Its absurd size in comparison with gaming minis is subtly hinted at by both cramming it inside the largest Rathcore painting handle, tacked to a painting base probably intended for horses*, and standing it next to an industry-standard reference pot of Vallejo plastic putty. *🤷‍♂️

item.title: 11:32 am, February 16, 2026
item.description: Just realised that my own ‘my roman empire’ may be the 2007 Dairy Milk gorilla ad.

item.title: 8:32 pm, February 13, 2026
item.description: Just look at that boopable snoot.

media:content.media:description: It’s Grimlock! All the little dings and divots of a Geoff Senior panel visible and magnificent.

item.title: 9:03 pm, February 12, 2026
item.description:
Yay!

We’re definitely into the golden age of the series now.

media:content.media:description: It’s Death’s Head! His first appearance, 16th May 1987, issue 113 of the Marvel UK Transformers run.
